result:
## Round 60 Goal

**Add a real-time metric correlation matrix overlay** — a compact 16×16 heatmap showing pairwise Pearson correlations between all 16 scalar metrics over a sliding window.

**Why this is high-value exploitation:** All 16 metrics already exist and are computed every frame. The anomaly detector watches them individually, the strange attractor plots 3 at a time, but nothing reveals *which metrics move together*. A correlation matrix instantly surfaces hidden relationships (e.g., does entropy track Lyapunov? does population predict complexity?) without the user needing to manually compare overlays. It turns the existing metric infrastructure into a relationship-discovery tool.

**Specifics:**
- Toggled with `)` or another free key
- 16×16 Braille/block heatmap: blue = anti-correlated, dark = uncorrelated, red = positively correlated
- 128-frame sliding window (matching anomaly detector)
- Metric labels on axes (abbreviated)
- Sidebar shows the top 3 strongest correlations and top 3 strongest anti-correlations as text
- Lightweight — just accumulate running sums/products of already-computed metrics

Shall I build it?
